===''Pokémon the Series''=== ====''[[Pokémon Journeys: The Series]]''==== [[File:Raihan anime.png|thumb|250px|Raihan in {{aniseries|JN}}]] Prior to his introduction to the series, Raihan entered the current [[World Coronation Series]] competition just to challenge {{an|Leon}}. During the current season, Raihan was skilled enough to grow rapidly in his rank, soon entering the Master Class. Raihan debuted in ''[[JN027|Toughing It Out!]]'', where he had a {{pkmn|battle}} against Leon during the [[World Coronation Series]] in [[Wyndon]]. His {{p|Duraludon}} battled [[Leon's Charizard]], only to be defeated after a tough battle between the two Trainers. After Leon's victory, Raihan reiterated his goal to defeat him. Raihan reappeared in a flashback in ''[[JN042|Sword and Shield, Slumbering Weald!]]''. In ''[[JN044|Sword and Shield: "From Here to Eternatus!"]]'', Raihan helped [[Sonia]] against a group of {{tc|Macro Cosmos's|Macro Cosmos employees}} who had accosted her outside the [[Energy Plant]] and [[Hammerlocke Stadium]]. In [[JN045|the following episode]], he easily defeated them and then left Sonia so he could take care of rampaging [[Dynamax]] Pokémon in the area. After [[Goh]] caught {{TP|Goh|Eternatus}} and the [[Darkest Day]] ended, he and Duraludon were seen looking on as the {{p|Kommo-o}} they were battling reverted back to its normal state. Raihan made a cameo appearance at the end of ''[[JN077|Ultra Exciting from the Shocking Start!]]'' while watching {{Ash}}'s World Coronation Series match against [[Volkner]]. In ''[[JN082|The Sweet Taste of Battle!]]'', Raihan met Ash and Goh when they all visited [[Ballonlea]] for [[Opal]]'s [[All-Alcremie Decoration Challenge]]. He was reluctant to participate, but did so anyway as Opal refused to let him just walk away. However, Raihan's cake ultimately lost to Ash's. He then stayed to watch the battle between Ash and Opal. After the battle, Raihan participated in eating the cakes decorated during the contest, finding Ash's cake very tasty. Raihan appeared in a fantasy in ''[[JN099|Battling Turned Up to Eleven!]]'', along with other Masters Eight Trainers. In ''[[JN107|Big Brother to the Rescue!]]'', Ash got notified that his next match would be up against Raihan. In ''[[JN108|Catching the Aura of Fate!]]'', Raihan noted his surprise that Ash had climbed through the ranks so quickly and began training for the match, declaring his intention to be the one to defeat Leon. Ash and Raihan battled at the Hammerlocke Stadium in ''[[JN109|Aim For The Eight!]]''. Utilizing his [[weather]] strategies, Raihan quickly started to overwhelm Ash. However, Raihan was ultimately defeated, causing his and Ash's ranks to swap places and Ash rising to the Master Class. Raihan took a commemorative photo with Ash and told him to not lose until he'd get a chance to defeat him and Leon. In ''[[JN129|A Flood of Torrential Gains!]]'', Raihan watched Ash's match against Leon. [[File:Leon and Raihan anime.png|thumb|250px|Raihan and Leon]] ====Character==== Like his game counterpart, Raihan has a strong rivalry with Leon, and it is the real reason he entered the World Coronation Series. As expected of a {{DL|World Coronation Series|Masters Eight}} member, Raihan is an exceptional {{pkmn|Trainer}}, being able to enter the Master Class shortly after the current season of the competition begins and is strong enough to knock out several Pokémon from [[Rose]]'s associates with a single move. Raihan is also very flirtatious, as seen when he defended [[Sonia]] from Rose's associates, during which he called her a "beautiful woman". After fending off Rose's crew, Raihan remarked that he would have asked Sonia out on a date if the circumstances weren't critical and also referred to her as a "lovely lady". This attitude of Raihan over time proved to be a disguise for his real feelings, so Raihan after being defeated by Leon started taking {{wp|Selfie|selfies}} to portray his true emotions. ====Pokémon==== This listing is of Raihan's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|PTS}}: =====On hand===== {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Raihan |pkmn=Duraludon |giga=yes |type1=Steel |type2=Dragon |img=Raihan Duraludon.png |cap=Raihan's Duraludon |img2=Raihan Gigantamax Duraludon.png |cap2=Raihan's Gigantamax Duraludon |epnum=JN027 |epname=Toughing It Out! |vajp=Yūichi Nakamura |vaen=Billy Kametz |vaennote=[[JN027]]-[[JN045]] |desc={{p|Duraludon}} is Raihan's first known Pokémon, as well as his ace. It is capable of [[Gigantamax]]ing. Raihan used it in his battle against {{an|Leon}}, where it went up against {{TP|Leon|Charizard}}, only to be defeated. In ''[[JN045|Sword and Shield... The Legends Awaken!]]'', Raihan used Duraludon to defeat the Pokémon of [[Oleana's subordinate]] and other [[Macro Cosmos]] {{tc|Macro Cosmos's|employees}}. Later, after the Darkest Day ended, Duraludon was seen battling a Dynamaxed {{p|Kommo-o}}. In ''[[JN109|Aim For The Eight!]]'', Duraludon was the final Pokémon used in Raihan's World Coronation Series match against {{Ash}}, where it went up against {{AP|Lucario}}. Despite its powerful [[Max Move]]s, Duraludon was defeated by Lucario's giant {{m|Aura Sphere}}. Duraludon's known moves are {{m|Metal Claw}}, {{m|Iron Defense}}, {{m|Stone Edge}}, {{m|Steel Beam}}, and {{m|Breaking Swipe}}.
As a Gigantamax Duraludon, it can use {{m|Max Rockfall}}, {{m|Max Steelspike}}, and {{m|G-Max Depletion}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Raihan |pkmn=Flygon |type1=Ground |type2=Dragon |img=Raihan Flygon.png |epnum=JN109 |epname=Aim For The Eight! |vajp= |vaen= |desc={{p|Flygon}} is Raihan's second known Pokémon. It was the first Pokémon that Raihan used in his [[World Coronation Series]] match against {{Ash}}, where it went up against his {{AP|Dragonite}} and won, before losing to [[Gigantamax]] {{AP|Gengar}}. Flygon's known moves are {{m|Dragon Claw}}, {{m|Steel Wing}}, {{m|Sandstorm}}, and {{m|Crunch}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Raihan |pkmn=Goodra |type1=Dragon |img=Raihan Goodra.png |epnum=JN109 |epname=Aim For The Eight! |vajp= |vaen= |desc={{p|Goodra}} is Raihan's third known Pokémon. It was the second Pokémon that Raihan used in his [[World Coronation Series]] match against {{Ash}}, where it went up against his [[Gigantamax]] {{AP|Gengar}}. The confrontation between the two Pokémon eventually led to a double knockout. Goodra's known moves are {{m|Rain Dance}}, {{m|Thunder}}, {{m|Surf}}, and {{m|Hydro Pump}}.}} =====Temporary===== {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Raihan |pkmn=Alcremie |gender=female |type1=Fairy |img=Raihan Alcremie.png |epnum=JN082 |epname=The Sweet Taste of Battle! |vajp= |vaen=Ryan Bartley |desc=Raihan borrowed an {{p|Alcremie}} from [[Opal]] to take part in the [[All-Alcremie Decoration Challenge]]. None of Alcremie's moves are known.}} =====Traveling with===== {| width="100%" |- | {{ActivePoké|||nick=Rotom Phone|Raihan Rotom Phone.png|electric|ghost|link=Rotom Phone}} | | |} ====Achievements==== [[File:Raihan Profile WCS.png|250px|thumb|Raihan's profile in the World Coronation Series]] =====World Coronation Series===== Raihan has competed in the [[World Coronation Series]]. His rank and class are as follows: * Rank 7{{tt|*|Dropped in rank after losing to Leon}} (Master Class; prior to ''[[JN027|Toughing It Out!]]'') * Rank unknown (Master Class; prior to ''[[JN100|Meeting Up with the Monarch!]]'') * Rank 8 (Master Class; prior to ''[[JN107|Big Brother to the Rescue!]]'') * Rank 9 (Ultra Class; ''[[JN109|Aim For The Eight!]]'') =====Pokémon competitions===== * [[All-Alcremie Decoration Challenge]] - Participant (''[[JN082|The Sweet Taste of Battle!]]'') ====Voice actors==== {{vatable|color={{dragon color}}|bordercolor={{dragon color dark}} |ja=鈴木達央 ''[[Tatsuhisa Suzuki]]'' ([[JN027]]-[[JN045]])
